chaos: time, destiny, knowledge, truth, uh I think everything?
aequor: the ocean, the abyss, including creatures, phenomena, and lost civilizations
caro: life, blood, and earthly desires
ultra: space-time, transendency (such as art), alien beings, and interdimension travelers

embeds.description: Heinrich Cornelius Agrippa von Nettesheim (; German: [aˈgʀɪpa]; 14 September 1486 – 18 February 1535) was a German Renaissance polymath, physician, legal scholar, soldier, knight, theologian, and occult writer.  Agrippa's Three Books of Occult Philosophy published in 1533 drew heavily upon Kabbalah,

the realms can be summarized somewhat with wheel of destiny lore lol

Chaos: 
“They do not discriminate, 
they do not differentiate, 
tears or rain, 
all are welcome, 
but none can leave.”

Aequor: 
“The King of Lemuria, our Lord, our liege. 
May the Gods forever bless you,
may your reign never cease."

Caro: 
“Close your eyes, oh weary one. 
Can't you taste the metallic tang of life? 
Relax, for you are safe, deep in the flesh's warm embrace."

Ultra: 
“Few can find the threshold, fewer can cross it and live.
But once you join the residents beyond the veil, you will be unbound. 
You will be free."
